title: waltheria cordata
intro: Waltheria cordata, also known as the Indian walnut or West Indian walnut, is a plant species in the family Sterculiaceae, native to tropical regions of Asia and Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Waltheria cordata include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
